principles: Usage consumption must be calculated from the existing observability plane and bounded inventory counts instead of from static quota configuration.
Every published usage snapshot must preserve tenant and workspace isolation and must never widen scope beyond the caller's authorization boundary.
Usage snapshots must remain trustworthy under telemetry degradation by marking freshness explicitly instead of silently presenting stale or missing evidence as current usage.
This baseline publishes the authoritative usage input layer for downstream quota policy, alerting, blocking, console, and verification work without preempting those later concerns.

freshness_states.label: Fresh
freshness_states.description: The underlying collection signals are current enough to be treated as the authoritative usage baseline.

freshness_states.label: Degraded
freshness_states.description: The latest evidence is present but older than the preferred cadence; consumers must surface the degraded state.

freshness_states.label: Unavailable
freshness_states.description: The usage evidence is missing or no trustworthy source was available for the dimension at calculation time.

rules: The default calculation cadence is five minutes unless a narrower operational profile overrides it.
Freshness is evaluated per dimension and summarized at snapshot level through the degradedDimensions list.
Unavailable dimensions must remain visible in the response catalog instead of being dropped from the snapshot.

metered_dimensions.description: Total REST and gateway-facing API requests attributed to the target scope during the observation window.
OpenWhisk-backed function invocations attributed to the target scope during the observation window.
Logical object-storage volume attributed to the target scope at snapshot time.
Aggregated PostgreSQL and MongoDB workload operations attributed to the target scope during the observation window.
Active realtime connection posture attributed to the target scope at snapshot time.
Exact count of logical PostgreSQL and MongoDB database resources bound to the target scope.
Exact count of governed Kafka topics bound to the target scope.
Exact count of tenant/workspace-managed MongoDB collections and PostgreSQL tables bound to the target scope.
Bounded error-count projection for workload and control-plane failures during the observation window, represented through normalized observability inputs rather than raw error payloads.

rules: startedAt must be earlier than or equal to endedAt.
All dimension observations in one snapshot share the same observation window unless a future task explicitly adds per-dimension override metadata.
Every snapshot must include every catalog dimension supported by the requested scope.
Dimensions with unavailable evidence remain present with value 0 and freshnessStatus unavailable unless a future task introduces a different explicit null policy.
The calculation-cycle audit summary must stay compatible with the canonical audit event schema and reuse the quota_metering subsystem id.
Workspace-scoped responses may reference only their own workspace while tenant-scoped responses may summarize tenant-wide processing for the requested tenant.
The audit summary must never embed raw per-request identifiers, secret material, or cross-tenant payload detail.
